This prevents repeated smoke runs from falsely thickening evidence. No scorer, route, paper/live, quota, max2, or final 9.26 claim is allowed.

holding_T_probe_outcome_join_review_only.first_read: v848 joins the v847 latest unique holding-T probe to review-only outcome fields without feeding outcomes back into runtime. The current T_PARTIAL_SELL evidence for 002491.SZ is blocked by three review gaps: event_time is a 15:00 end-of-day snapshot rather than an actionable intraday trigger, there are no same-day bars after the event, and local data has no next-session outcome after 2026-04-29. It still quantifies the prior intraday fade from same-day high to event price at about 2.23%, but the next step must be a first-trigger intraday logger rather than a rule or scorer.

holding_T_intraday_first_trigger_logger_.first_read: v849 upgrades the holding-day T runtime logger from EOD snapshot only to first-trigger diagnostics. The latest smoke for 002491.SZ records a first high-fade/lost-VWAP trigger at 2026-04-29 09:35 with price 16.48, drawdown from rolling high about -1.61%, and VWAP distance about -0.28%. It still keeps action_plan_rows=0, executed_rows=0, and all route/live/paper/training permissions false. The new blocker is early-open noise: the first trigger is too close to the open, so the next step is a no-route maturity gate rather than a sell rule.

holding_T_first_trigger_maturity_gate_no.first_read: v850 adds a no-route maturity gate over the holding-day T first-trigger logger. The current 002491.SZ first-trigger row is classified as OPEN_NOISE_OBSERVE because the first trigger is 2026-04-29 09:35 and lacks mature confirmation, so it cannot become a mature partial-sell probe. This preserves the core holding-T lesson: T frequency exists, but sell/buyback/keep-reduced arbitration needs same-schema historical evidence before any scorer, route, paper, or live change.

holding_T_historical_same_schema_backfil.first_read: v851 backfills historical v843 holding-day T rows into the v846/v849/v850 runtime sidecar shape without training or routing. It produces 461 same-schema rows, including 451 train/exam rows and 197 T_PARTIAL_SELL rows, while quarantining all review-only outcome fields. The first read is that holding-day T has enough evidence for arbitration review, but missing micro context such as VWAP, sellable quantity, slope, and reclaim bars blocks any route promotion. Next step is action decomposition: partial-sell-only vs buyback vs keep-reduced.

holding_T_sell_buyback_keep_reduced_deco.first_read: v852 decomposes holding-day T into partial sell, buyback, and keep-reduced paths. In 2023-2025 train/exam rows, 108 completed buybacks split into only 24 confirmed buybacks, 41 counterfactual keep-reduced rows, and 43 thin-spread buybacks. This confirms completed buyback cannot be treated as success by default. The next T step is to find current-visible proxies for buyback risk, while dynamic sell/exit quality remains the larger 9.26 gap contributor.

holding_T_current_visible_buyback_risk_p.first_read: v853 audits current-visible coarse proxy fields against v852 review-only buyback risk labels. It finds a few risk/confirm proxy candidates, but the contract still blocks route promotion because key micro context is incomplete: VWAP distance, reclaim bars, last-3 amount slope, last-3 low lift, and sector context. This closes the current T sub-loop as evidence-only and pushes the main 9.26 work back toward dynamic sell/exit quality.

dynamic_exit_quality_reprioritization_au.first_read: v854 closes the current T sub-loop as evidence-only and moves the main 9.26 path back to dynamic exit quality. The older v360 dynamic sell suite shows large uplift but needs clean revalidation; the more conservative v364 gated sell averages about +0.6813pp weekly in 2023-2025 at 200k; the more realistic v462 exit-day regret audit averages about +0.2452pp. Next step is an active-winner/cash-release/current-visible exit regret ledger, not route promotion.

dynamic_exit_active_winner_cash_release_.first_read: v855 turns v462 exit-day dynamic sell evidence into an active-winner/cash-release ledger. At 200k it scans 232 exit rows and finds 32 dynamic candidates, 28 in 2023-2025 train/exam, 18 with a reusable cash window, 28 weak/fade protection rows, and 1 active-winner risk row. This supports dynamic exit as a no-route mainline research direction, but the next step must be a current-visible gate contract, not order emission.

dynamic_exit_current_visible_gate_contra.first_read: v856 derives a no-route current-visible dynamic-exit gate contract from v855. It deliberately blocks review-only edge fields from the gate. On v855 rows it splits 32 dynamic candidates into 26 CASH_RELEASE_PROTECT_REVIEW rows and 6 BLOCK_ACTIVE_WINNER_REVIEW rows, with 6 missing runtime context blockers still listed. Next step is a runtime shadow logger design that records the missing post-trigger sector/reclaim/cash-queue fields without changing exits.

dynamic_exit_runtime_shadow_logger_desig.first_read: v857 designs a log-only runtime shadow logger for dynamic exit. It is position-driven, maps all six v856 missing runtime contexts to planned sources, finds all six intended hook points in run_ths_sim_once_v1.py, and keeps a 10-row permission contract blocking action_plan, sell_plan, buy_plan, official_buy, repair_weak quota, max2, paper/live orders, and training.

dynamic_exit_runtime_shadow_logger_patch.first_read: v858 patches run_ths_sim_once_v1.py with a position-driven dynamic-exit runtime shadow logger and smoke-validates it in read_only mode. The smoke produced one dynamic_exit_runtime_shadow_sidecar row for 002491.SZ with CASH_RELEASE_PROTECT_REVIEW, while official_buy_rows, action_plan_rows, executed_rows, repair_weak quota, route, paper, live, and training all stayed at zero/false.

dynamic_exit_runtime_shadow_daily_accumu.first_read: v859 seeds the daily accumulator for dynamic_exit_runtime_shadow_sidecar rows. It confirms the no-route logger can be collected, but only one distinct trade date exists so far. The next blockers are field completeness: raw held positions currently lack entry/cost context, and the seed row lacks post-trigger sector context.

next_recommended_step.purpose: fill entry/cost context and post-trigger sector context for dynamic-exit runtime shadow rows without changing exits or orders
next_recommended_step.parallel_track: holding-day T remains evidence-only until missing micro context is filled; candidate supply remains no-route scout/ranker evidence only
hard_constraints: no action scorer or ranker training
no next-open or future outcome as runtime feature
no final 9.26 claim from T review outputs
historical outcome labels must remain review-only and quarantined from runtime feature surface
action decomposition must not treat completed buyback as success by default
dynamic exit runtime shadow logger must write logs only and must not emit exit orders

candidate_common_horizon_oracle_v2.first_read: v824 recomputes skipped/source-only candidate supply against active references using the same samples_short_v2 09:40 entry plus 3-trading-day close exit horizon. The skipped-candidate gap remains positive and has 5 shadow-review-ready pockets, but raw pre-v485 source-only supply is negative across train/valid/frozen common-horizon summaries and no independent second-source pocket is ready. This is a supply ceiling diagnostic only, not route, max2, scorer training, quota, paper/live, or a final 9.26 claim.

candidate_actionability_ladder.first_read: v825 converts v824 evidence into actionability. Skipped-candidate common-horizon pockets have replay-worthy edge but need slot/replacement replay and active route overlap; raw pre-v485 source-only supply is blocked as direct supply because common-horizon edge is negative across train/valid/frozen. No independent second-source pocket, max2, route, paper/live, quota, or scorer training is allowed. Parallel evidence path is holding intraday T human-label batch.

skipped_candidate_slot_replacement_repla.first_read: v826 tests v825 ready skipped-candidate pockets under max1 replacement, not max2. Rank-first replacement is positive in train/valid/frozen common-horizon buckets, while oracle-best is much higher. This is the first stronger supply/actionability signal after v824, but it remains no-route because active route overlap, conflict replay, cash/slot mechanics, and promotion gates are not yet complete.

active_route_overlap_bridge_for_v793.first_read: v827 joins v826 ready skipped-candidate rows to daily_priority_book/buy_signal active route proxies after preserving active_trade_date through v824. Active book proxy coverage is now 100%, and lower-conflict row-level edge is positive, but rank-first lower-conflict daily replay remains weak/unstable. The strong v826 edge appears conflict-heavy or active-route-dependent, so no route, paper/live, max2, quota, scorer, or promotion is allowed.

active_route_proxy_source_native_backfil.first_read: v828 uses source-native active_family/active_subphase backfill to keep active-route proxy coverage at 100%. It confirms the coverage issue is solved, but daily lower-conflict replay is still weak, so the candidate replacement edge is not yet a clean low-conflict independent second source. Next work should either collect holding intraday T human labels or filter the lower-conflict pocket further; no route, paper/live, max2, quota, scorer training, or final 9.26 claim is allowed.

holding_intraday_t_human_label_batch.first_read: v829 turns the user's existing-position intraday T idea into a batchable human label ledger and minute-enrichment protocol. It creates a 20-row manual label template with the 002491.SZ case as a pending seed, keeps exact sell/buyback prices blank instead of inferring them from screenshots, links the v748 seed-day micro context, and blocks T scorer/rule design until at least 20 completed broker-label rows exist. No route, paper/live, same-symbol T route, quota, max2, scorer training, or final 9.26 claim is allowed.

lower_conflict_replacement_filter.first_read: v830 diagnoses why lower-conflict replacement looks strong row-by-row but weak in daily replay. The unfiltered lower-conflict daily replay is still negative in 2025 valid, mainly because most days have no lower-conflict candidate and selected negative days are large. Current-visible/proxy filters produce 10 thin review-ready filters, led by v194_dynamic_two_slot_reserve / v198_r010 style filters, but these are not promotion-ready until cash/slot mechanics, manual review, and promotion gates pass. Selection remains rank-first within filters and does not sort by future edge; no route, paper/live, max2, quota, scorer training, or final 9.26 claim is allowed.

replacement_cash_slot_mechanics.first_read: v831 applies 100-share lot, cash floor, slot, and same-day active replacement mechanics to the v830 review-ready filters. The lot/cash floor is not the main blocker, but strict same-day max1 replacement is not ready: no strict scenario passes train/valid after mechanics, and the broad positive edge partly depends on prior-holding rotation timing. This blocks promotion of v826/v830 replacement edge and points the next mainline toward trade-frequency elasticity and temporal/rotation anatomy. No route, paper/live, max2, quota, scorer training, or final 9.26 claim is allowed.

trade_frequency_elasticity_audit.first_read: v832 confirms that trade frequency and candidate supply are major bottlenecks. In 2023-2025 at the 200k clean baseline, current frequency averages about 1.37 trades/week; keeping current per-trade edge would require about 4.03 trades/week on average to approach 9.26. v540 final rejected rows are too few to solve this, while pre-v485 source-only supply has 660 date-symbol keys and is the only large enough new-symbol supply layer. Holding-day T has meaningful action frequency, but needs human broker labels before rules/scoring. No route, paper/live, repair_weak quota, max2, model training, or final 9.26 claim is allowed.

pre_v485_common_horizon_scout_slot_repla.first_read: v833 tests pre-v485 source-only/review pockets under the same common 09:40 plus 3 trading-day horizon and a scout-slot interpretation. Four scenarios have standalone scout edge across train/valid/frozen, led by S1_v802_review_rank_first, but zero scenarios have positive replacement edge versus the active clean trade. Therefore pre-v485 can continue as scout-slot supply research, not max1 replacement, max2, or route. Rank-first one-per-day chosen dates still do not cover the full trade-frequency gap by themselves, so the next step is a current-plus-scout frequency/return proxy combined later with holding intraday T and exit-speed work. No route, paper/live, repair_weak quota, max2, model training, or final 9.26 claim is allowed.

replacement_mechanics_matrix.first_read: v834 completes the A/B/C/D replacement mechanics matrix requested by the latest guidance: rank-first, filter-first, rank-then-filter-veto, and active-weakness replacement, each under broad lot, strict same-day, and notional-neutral mechanics. Strict same-day replacement is not promotable: even when mean edge is positive, train/valid all-date positive rates are only about 35% and 30%, tail loss is large, and train replaces active winners too often. Broad edge remains scout/rotation research only. No route, paper/live, repair_weak quota, max2, scorer training, or final 9.26 claim is allowed.

low_conflict_negative_day_active_winner_.first_read: v835 splits negative replacement days into candidate-source, rank-first, active-original-stronger, and timing/action drivers. In strict same-day train/valid rows, the main blocker is not merely low-conflict filtering: many negative rows are bad candidates and many also replace active winners, while rank-first mistakes are present but not the only issue. This points next toward scout gate/ranker input design and active-strength veto design, not route or max2. Same-horizon realized returns remain review labels only; no route, paper/live, repair_weak quota, max2, scorer training, or final 9.26 claim is allowed.

ranker_or_scout_gate_design_no_training.first_read: v836 converts v834/v835 replacement failures into a no-training scout gate and ranker input contract. It whitelists 31 current-visible/proxy fields, locks 11 realized/oracle fields as review-only labels, and maps 41 scout-gate cells into source/active veto, ranker-required, scout-keep, or observe-more buckets. The main blocker remains replacement safety: bad candidates, rank-first mistakes, and replacing active winners are all present, so the next step is a no-training scout-gate shadow replay rather than route, max2, paper/live, quota change, or model training.

scout_gate_shadow_replay_no_training.first_read: v837 applies the v836 no-training scout gate to v828 pre-v485 candidate rows using rank-first selection after gate, never future-edge sorting. The ungated rank-first reference scenario is cross-bucket positive as scout shadow, but the actual v836 gated scenarios fail cross-bucket readiness because the 2026 frozen candidate leg is negative. This means pre-v485 supply still deserves a current-plus-scout frequency proxy, but the v836 gate is not yet a收益增强器 and replacement remains blocked. No route, paper/live, repair_weak quota, max2, scorer training, or final 9.26 claim is allowed.

current_plus_scout_shadow_frequency_retu.first_read: v838 combines the current clean frequency target table with v837 scout shadow rows as a no-route frequency proxy. The best scout reference scenario has positive review returns across 2023-2025, but only adds 18 scout dates and covers about 6.01% of the frequency gap on average. This confirms pre-v485 scout supply is directionally useful but far too thin to be the 9.26 engine by itself; next work should expand scout supply safely or bridge holding-day intraday T action frequency after human labels. No return claim, replacement, max2, route, paper/live, repair_weak quota, model training, or final 9.26 claim is allowed.

expand_scout_supply_or_add_holding_T_fre.first_read: v839 expands the v838 scout reference into wider pre-v485 source-only supply and replaces the prior human-label T blocker with machine self-labels from v369 holding-day T rows, per the latest instruction. Wide pre-v485 rank-first supply can cover the frequency gap on date count, but edge quality is thin: all-source rank-first has 388 main-period scout dates and about 108.95% gap coverage, yet only about 1.14% mean review return and about 51.8% positive rate; the v802 review subset is cleaner but smaller, with 216 dates and about 62.41% gap coverage. Machine T self-labels add 197 sell-label rows, with 190 actions in 2023-2025 and about 53.65% action-frequency coverage; good-spread and next-open-risk labels are now available for logic discovery. No route, paper/live, repair_weak quota, max2, model training, or final 9.26 claim is allowed.

active_winner_protection_gate.first_read: v840 answers the active-winner part of the v835 failure map before any new replacement attempt. Using only current-visible/proxy active context as gate inputs and keeping realized/oracle fields as review labels, it catches all 24 train/valid active_original_stronger rows inside 28 protected strict same-day rows. It leaves zero train/valid rows as replacement-allowed after conservative conflict resolution, so pre-v485 remains scout/review only. Next step is v841 candidate source hygiene, because active protection alone does not clean source pollution or rank-first mistakes. No route, paper/live, repair_weak quota, max2, scorer training, or final 9.26 claim is allowed.

pre_v485_candidate_source_hygiene_gate.first_read: v841 treats the v835 candidate_source_bad driver as source hygiene, not another replacement filter. It creates 178 no-route hygiene cells from current-visible pre-v485 source/micro/context fields, with 24 block-source-bad cells and 10 keep-source-review cells. After hygiene, rank-first scout coverage falls from about 108.95% of the 2023-2025 frequency gap to about 38.24%, but review mean return improves from about 1.14% to about 2.22% and positive rate improves from about 51.8% to about 59.7%. This is cleaner scout evidence, not a route/paper/live permission. Next step is v842 pairwise ranker input audit, because hygiene does not solve rank-first mistakes or replacement arbitration.

pairwise_ranker_input_audit_no_training.first_read: v842 converts the replacement/scout problem into a no-training pairwise audit set. It builds 407 pairwise rows over 190 trade dates: 84 active-vs-replacement strict same-day rows, 85 rank-first-vs-actual-best source rows, and 238 rank-first-vs-each-candidate rows. Current-visible fields from v840 active protection and v841 source hygiene show useful separators for source-bad, active-winner, rank-first-wrong, and positive pairwise pockets, but this is audit-only; no scorer/ranker is trained and no route/paper/live permission is granted. Next step moves to v843 holding-day intraday T replay under current v540/v590 semantics.
